rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF ROWAN STRUMMER TELLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, allowing (not approving) the accounting, although the amended verification referred to the wrong filing date of this second amended petition, it is unclear what is meant by "unrealized loss" of securities, the summary of account shows Schedule D as -0-, the purchase price ($21,914.57) for the 8 shares of Alphabet must be incorrect, if the carry value shown on Schedule E ($21,944.57) is correct, the attorney's fee declaration wss neither dated nor signed, and it is unclear why only $8,199.00 was paid to Ernest Der on 12/21/21 for fees not actually awarded until ordered on 4/5/22, which order granted him $8,611.50 + costs of $915.00. Guardian invested funds in securities without prior Court approval. [LRSF 14.46 D.5.] Guardian must petition for authority before investing any guardianship funds in mutual funds or if wishing to change the securities on hand at the end of this accounting period. Fourth accounting must clearly address the liabilities/fees paid to Ernest Der.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EVA C. TOLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/6/24. (No earlier availability and to follow status on filing receipt of the blocked account.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice is defective if mailed before the pleading requiring notice is e-filed. [LRSF 14.14 A.] The second amended petition was e-filed on 1/8/24. (2) The Notice of Hearing must include the full caption of the pleading to be heard. (3) Several attorney time entries (Mr. Musacchio's .4 hours on 3/27/23 and .9 hours on 10/12/22 and Ms. Hoffman's entries on 9/14/22, 3/27/23, 4/20/23, 6/28/23, 7/18/23, 7/19/23) appear to have been duplicated, and the category for 10/12/22 on page 8 of the fee declaration was for .9 hours, or $360, not 1.6 hours for $640. Ms. Hoffman's time entry on 9/5/22 appears to be a duplicate of Mr. Musacchio's time on the same date. Other entries also appear to be duplicative; it is unclear why two attorneys needed to be involved in the real property sale. This Court does not ordinarily grant fees for internal conferences. Time spent to discuss the estate's bank account with a personal representative is ordinary. (4) The fee declarations do not appear to fully comply with CRC 7.702. Deadline is 2/26/24 for providing the assigned examiner the courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KONRAD KRAUSE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/5/24. (No earlier availability.) 1. Proof of service shows notice mailed on 12/14/23, but the accounting was not e-filed until 12/18/23. Notice is defective if mailed before the pleading requiring notice is e-filed. [LRSF 14.14 A.] (It is unclear why the accounting was filed separately and not as part of the Petition for Settlement of First and Final Account, etc.) 2. The bank statement for the savings account is for the period ending 9/26/23. The statement verifying the ending balance must encompass, not precede, the ending date of the accounting. (It is noted that well over the FDIC amount was maintained at just one bank.) Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 2/23/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: The disbursements schedule must a full description of each payment: "IBM Pension Plan" without more is not really adequate. Although the attorney costs were helpfully described as to what was e-filed, etc., it would be more helpful if the dates were shown. A petitioner does not need to give notice to himself. When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 2-1/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders. In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" or "Respondent" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARY ANN TONKIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/27/2024. The proof of publication filed on 01/12/2024 indicates that the initial publication was made less than 15 days before the last hearing date specified in the published notice. Also, all names by which the decedent was known (see item 1 of the DE-121 form on file) must be published. New notice must be published. In addition, the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 01/02/2024) remain unaddressed: 1) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-121 form at least 15 days before the hearing. 2) Item 3g(1)(d) of the petition must be completed re the first-named executor. The date of death must be provided if applicable. 3) Item 8: Notice to Lynn Hamrick, Pamela Brown, and Galen Brown must be given to their place of business or residence (street address instead of P.O. Box must be confirmed by verified allegation). See section 1215(a)(3).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARGARET A. FOOTE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/5/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No WILL FILE has been created yet, as neither the originals of the will and codicil have been lodged nor copies filed. (2) The "corrected amended" petition filed on 12/20/23 (which should have been captioned "second amended" petition, etc.) is incomplete in the caption and item 2c if IAEA authority is being requested. (3) Item 3f(3) of the "corrected amended" petition is checked, indicating that the will and/or codicil have been lost. If they are lost, the pleading caption and both mailed and published notice must clearly state this. [LRSF 14.20.] (4) Item 4 of the "corrected amended" petition is blank. (5) The proof of service shows that notice was mailed to five individuals who do not appear to be interested parties and no notice to six individuals who were listed in item 8 of the petition. Deadline is 2/23/24 for providing courtesy copies to the assigned examiner. If the will and codicil are lost, a copy of the lost testamentary documents must be attached to the proposed order. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. ("Succ. executor & trustee" is not a relationship to the decedent; one is either kin or a stranger.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE HATIE LEE JOHNSON REVOCABLE TRUST DATED SEPTEMber 7, 2012, an irrevocable non testementary
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Disinherit And/Or Disqualify Respondent Trust Benificiary And Alternative Trustee Pursuant To California Probate Code Section 21311; Pargr
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/19/2024. The following issues (e-mailed to petitioners in pro per on 01/29/2024) must be addressed: 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 30 days before the hearing. 3) Patricia Johnson's street address as her place of residence or business must be provided by a verified allegation. Notice must be given to that address. See Probate Code section 1215(a)(3). 4) Direct notice must be given to Patricia Johnson, absent her signed directive stating that notice to her attorney is sufficient. See CRC 7.51(a)(1) & (2). 5) Please explain why the Court should not stay or dismiss the petition under CCP section 410.30 in favor of the pending proceeding in Nevada. 6) Did Patricia Johnson's petition for instructions filed in the Nevada proceeding (Exhibit E) allege any ground listed in section 21310(b) as a direct contest of the trust? If not, please explain why section 21311(a)(2) or (3) should apply, as paragraph 7.4 of the trust does not appear to provide for that application.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JORGE EFRAIN MONROY REVOCABLE living TRUST DATED APRIl 3, 2017, as amended
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Title To Property (Probate Code Section 17200.1. 850 Set Seq)
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/12/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The incorrect form of Notice of Hearing was served. (2) Prob. C. Sect. 850 succeeded Heggstad and sets higher standards for proving that assets not transferred to a trust during a trustor's life were, in fact, intended to be held in the trust. In other words, a beneficiary of the trust receives mailed notice with a copy of the petition, but any heir-at-law, who would stand to inherit, if the trust did not exist or were invalid, is a party whose right, title or interest would be affected by this petition, and, therefore, must receive (or waive) personal service of notice. [Prob. C. Sect. 17203(b) and CCP Sect. 413.10 et seq.] It appears that the predeceased son, Jorge Efrain Monroy, Jr., was survived by at least one daughter. Deadline is 3/1/24 for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
